personal.name: Marjorie Schwarzer
personal.title: Museum Studies Educator, Author & Critic
personal.subtitle: Professor Emeritus and Founding Administrative Director of the Museum Studies Graduate Program at the University of San Francisco (USF)
personal.bio: Marjorie Schwarzer is a prominent American museum studies educator, author, and critic. She served as the founding Administrative Director of the Museum Studies graduate program at the University of San Francisco (USF) until her retirement in 2020. Prior to USF, she chaired the Department of Museum Studies at John F. Kennedy University for over 15 years. Her research and publications focus on the history and evolution of museums in America, museology, and decolonization of cultural institutions. She was awarded the Western Museums Association (WMA) Leadership Award in 2018.

books.title: Riches, Rivals, and Radicals: 100 Years of Museums in America
books.publisher: Rowman & Littlefield / American Alliance of Museums
books.description: An essential historical exploration of American museums over the past century, detailing how they evolved from private cabinets of curiosity to major public centers of education, civic engagement, and community reflection.

books.title: What Students Need to Know: Graduate Training in Museum Studies
books.description: A comprehensive overview of museum studies training, guiding museum career pathways, certification, and academic training frameworks.
